definition: A pricing strategy where basic features are offered free of charge while advanced features require a paid subscription.
extendedDefinition: Freemium models reduce the barrier to entry by letting users experience core functionality without upfront payment. Users naturally upgrade as their needs grow, making it an effective lead-generation engine for SaaS companies. The challenge lies in balancing free features that provide real value against paid features that are compelling enough to convert users. Successful freemium products like Dropbox and Spotify have used this model to grow from zero to millions of paying customers.
